title: WorldPop Saudi Arabia Gridded Population Density, 2000-2020
abstract: Annual 1 km resolution gridded population density rasters for Saudi Arabia (2000-2020), following WorldPop naming and grid conventions. Provides a spatially explicit, country-wide view of population distribution that can be clipped to Red Sea coastal governorates for human-pressure and coastal-development analyses.

resources.name: Annual population density grids, Saudi Arabia (GeoTIFF, 21 files)

variables.name: population_density
variables.label: Population Density
variables.description: Estimated number of people per square kilometer per 1km grid cell, one raster layer per year from 2000 to 2020.

steward.name: WorldPop Research Group, University of Southampton

access.how_to_request: Freely downloadable from https://www.worldpop.org/ under open license; no request required.

provenance.originator: WorldPop, School of Geography and Environmental Science, University of Southampton
provenance.citation: WorldPop (www.worldpop.org). Saudi Arabia population density grids, 1km resolution, 2000-2020.

known_issues: File naming (sau_pd_{year}_1km.tif) and directory context strongly match WorldPop's population density product, but the exact WorldPop sub-product (constrained vs. unconstrained, UN-adjusted vs. not) could not be confirmed from the files alone — original download provenance/README not present in the folder.
Coverage is all of Saudi Arabia, not clipped to Red Sea coastal governorates; no Red-Sea-specific subsetting has been performed.
No data for 2021-2024 (Rmd registry entry marks WorldPop as [Previous]/already acquired, but the local archive stops at 2020).
Exact citation DOI not confirmed for this specific product/vintage, so left null rather than guessed.
